audience: Engineers building or integrating multi-agent AI systems

mode_descriptions.single_agent: One orchestrator LLM handles all tool calls directly
mode_descriptions.multi_agent: Orchestrator delegates to specialized sub-agents; each has its own tool set

flows.name: Search + Synthesize
flows.purpose: User asks a question that requires web search. Agent retrieves, stores in memory, and synthesizes.
step_outline: user → orch     : user message with question
orch → memory   : retrieve relevant prior context
orch → search   : tool_use / delegate to search agent
orch → memory   : store new results for future
orch → user     : synthesized answer with citations

flows.name: Code Execution
flows.purpose: User asks to analyze data or run code. Agent writes and executes in a sandbox.
step_outline: orch → code     : tool_use code_execution / delegate to code agent
code → code     : execute in sandbox, capture output
orch → user     : formatted analysis with code snippet

flows.name: Multi-Tool Chain
flows.purpose: Complex query requiring search, code, and memory in sequence.

lessons: Single-agent vs multi-agent is an excellent mode pair: same user story, different internal routing. The orchestrator node changes from 'does everything' to 'routes to specialists'.
Memory (vector store) appears in most flows as both a read (retrieve context) and a write (store results). This makes it a central participant even when it's not the protagonist.
Tool calls and agent delegation look identical from the user's perspective. The mode toggle reveals the internal difference.
